fix: correct Apollo webhook payload format and add source_id matching

Apollo sends phone data in `people[]` array (not `person` object).
Our webhook handler was reading the wrong key, causing empty payloads.

Changes:
- Add `source_id` column to leads table for Apollo person ID storage
- Store Apollo person ID during import for webhook matching
- Rewrite webhook handler to read `people[]` format per Apollo docs
- Match leads by source_id (primary) with email fallback
- Handle both new and legacy payload formats

commit.message:
fix(desktop): disable SSL verification for CDN attachment downloads

Oracle Cloud VM has SSL interception on cdn.signal.org connections,
causing CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED errors. Attachments are already
encrypted with Signal's own AES-256-CBC + HMAC-SHA256, so TLS
verification on the CDN transport is not a security concern.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(cancel): EMAIL_FROM statt EMAIL_USER als Absender-Adresse

EMAIL_USER ist der SMTP-Login, nicht die Absender-Adresse. Der SMTP-Server
hat die FROM-Adresse mit 501 rejected. Jetzt wird EMAIL_FROM verwendet,
genau wie im funktionierenden mailer.js Service.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add explicit find-vs-grep rule to sys1 prompt

Examples alone are not enough for a 1.5B model to reliably distinguish
filename search from content search. Add a two-line rule directly in
the prompt that spells out the distinction:

  File by NAME (called, named)         → find -iname
  File by CONTENT (contains word, has text inside) → grep -ril

Small models follow explicit rules more reliably than inferring them
from few-shot examples. This should fix recurring "contains word X"
queries being misrouted to find -iname instead of grep -ril.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(api): Fix double-protocol bug in storage health indicator URL

STORAGE_ENDPOINT can be a full URL (https://...) or a bare hostname.
The health indicator always prepended a protocol, causing malformed
URLs like http://https://... when using Supabase S3 endpoints.
Now auto-detects whether the endpoint already has a protocol.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix sticky: poll and re-apply while mpv runs instead of applying twice

XFCE unpredictably clears _NET_WM_STATE_STICKY whenever mpv remaps its
window (e.g. on video load, resize). Timing two applications is fragile.

Instead, _apply_sticky now runs a 1-second polling loop for the entire
mpv session: check the window state with xprop and re-apply with wmctrl
whenever XFCE has cleared it. Exits cleanly when the IPC socket goes away.

commit.message:
fix: audit protocol — 7 bugs causing false positives

Root cause: WebFetch converts HTML to markdown, losing <head> content
(meta tags, canonical, JSON-LD, alt attributes). Agent reported
"missing" items it never actually checked.

Fixed:
- audit.md: 4-phase protocol (collect → check → score → self-check)
  - Phase 1: explicit tool selection (Scrapling raw HTML > WebFetch)
  - Phase 2: robots.txt fetched separately, sitemap path from robots.txt
  - Phase 3: security headers marked [NOT VERIFIED] when no curl access
  - Phase 4: hard gate self-check before delivering report
- SKILL.md: tool selection table, "WebFetch Trap" warning, enforced
  validation protocol with data source citations
- schema-generator.md: raw HTML required for JSON-LD extraction
- data-sources.md: honest tool capability matrix (what each CAN/CANNOT do)

Also added: agent definition, full skill set (7 files), updated README.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fuse gate+up GEMV into single dispatch with split writer kernel

Reduces FFN chain from 6 to 5 programs per layer by combining gate and up
projections into a single GEMV that writes to two separate output buffers
via a split writer kernel. Saves one program boundary + one activation
read per layer × 32 layers (~1ms/tok improvement).

commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Disable proxy-level auth check, rely on page-level auth

Session cookie is confirmed set but Edge Middleware can't read it
on navigation. Each protected page already checks auth via
getUserWithProjects() and redirects to /sign-in if unauthenticated.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Turn 2 ignoring valid search results + wrong command for content search

Root cause: find -iname searches FILENAMES not file contents. For "file
containing word X", grep -ril is correct. find -iname is for "file named X".

Changes:
- sys1: add grep example for Downloads to anchor "contains word X" → grep
  (previously only Desktop+Documents had grep examples; Downloads was missing,
  so LLM matched Downloads queries to find -iname instead)
- reformatOutput: add handlers for plain find (filename results) and grep -r
  (content results) — both add a labeled header so Turn 2 knows what the
  data represents instead of seeing raw unlabelled filenames
- sys2: rewrite from hedging ("if data doesn't answer, say I don't have it")
  to directive ("report what the data shows") — stops LLM from asking
  clarifying questions when it receives unlabelled output
- usr2: reinstate command context "Command output (from `cmd`):" so Turn 2
  always knows what command produced the data
- Side effect: header line bumps allLines count from 4→5, so clipboard
  copy now fires correctly for 4-result searches

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Suggest Edit and Copy Link interactive features

Select any text to get a floating toolbar with two options:

1. Suggest Edit — opens a modal to write a suggested change, then
   submits it as a structured GitHub Issue (no auth or git knowledge
   needed). Issue includes original text, suggestion, section context.

2. Copy Link — generates a scroll-to-text-fragment URL (#:~:text=)
   and copies to clipboard. Recipients get the text auto-highlighted
   and scrolled to when they open the link.

ever had this week's items. Now:

- Daily digest crawl widened from 3 to 14 days for ongoing coverage
- Blocked sender filtering applied to both pipelines

commit.message:
Bisection drawdown : capital épuisé exactement à ageFin

Le drawdown était calculé par formule d'annuité avec un taux blend fixe,
mais la simulation applique des rendements distincts par enveloppe avec
des drags fiscaux différents. Résultat : dérive cumulative (ex: 426k€
de résidu à 80 ans au lieu de 0).

Fix : simulateDrawdownFinal() réplique la logique capital des phases 2-3
(croissance par enveloppe, retrait pro-rata, IS drag, PER débloqué à 64).
Bisection (50 itérations max, convergence à 1€) sur cette simulation
pour trouver le drawdown exact. La formule d'annuité reste comme borne
haute initiale.

commit.message:
Add per-node environment variables for API keys and secrets

Each workflow node now supports key-value env vars stored in node config. In the dashboard, a visual editor with password masking lets you add/remove/toggle-visibility of env vars. In Telegram, a 🔑 Env Vars button on node detail lets you add (KEY=value format) and remove vars. Scripts access them as `env.KEY` in JavaScript or `{{KEY}}` in LLM prompts. Config merging preserves existing node settings when updating env vars.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(web): fix auth redirect loop after login

Two issues fixed:
1. Renamed proxy.ts → lib/auth-middleware-draft.ts to prevent it from
   being compiled as Next.js middleware. It was causing 307 redirects
   from /dashboard to /auth?redirect= because the cookie check always
   fails (cookies are on the API domain, not the web domain).

2. Dashboard layout had a zustand persist hydration race condition:
   isAuthenticated defaults to false before localStorage rehydration,
   causing immediate redirect to /auth. Added hydration gate to wait
   for persist rehydration before checking auth state.

Fix base SCPI (réinvestissements) + prévoyance phase 2 + documenter limitations

1. Base comptable SCPI : les distributions réinvesties (après IS et frais
   de souscription) augmentent maintenant la base. Évite la double taxation
   partielle à la cession (~3-5% d'erreur corrigée sur la fiscalité SCPI).

2. Prévoyance ajoutée aux frais phase 2 (levée de pied) : la SASU maintenue
   avec missions ponctuelles garde la prévoyance (~3k€/an).

3. Documenter les limitations assumées :
   - Forfait TME déduit du contrat capi (pas de la tréso SASU)
   - IR foyer résiduel non modélisé en phases 2-3

commit.message:
refactor: rename internal demo naming from quick/guided to flash/deep (#187)

Internal demo code now uses 'deep'/'flash' consistently instead of
'guided'/'quick' for session types, matching the user-facing labels.

- _lastSessionType: 'guided'|'quick' → 'deep'|'flash'
- _nextFlightType: 'quick' → 'flash'
- setQuickTuneMode() → setFlashTuneMode()
- setGuidedTuneMode() → setDeepTuneMode()
- generateQuickDemoBBL() → generateFlashDemoBBL()
- StressScenario.flightType: 'quick' → 'flash'

Note: TuningType in shared types remains 'guided'|'quick' for backward
compatibility with serialized user data.
